hooks.text: You offered your supplier a credit card, the safest way to pay. To your supplier, that card is the riskiest thing you could have sent.
Your supplier didn't say no to your money. Your supplier said no to your card. There is one word on it that scares every factory.
Out of every hundred Chinese suppliers, only three to five take a credit card. The card you assumed was an option mostly is not.
A business owner pulled out a Visa, expecting a yes. The supplier asked for a bank wire instead. Same order, same money, one quiet rejection.
Every card payment hands your supplier a six percent cut and a buyer who can claw it back for one hundred eighty days.
